revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:池上七福神]]


'''{{ill|Ikegami Shichifukujin|ja|池上七福神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikegami Shichifukujin|qq=}}''' is a '''Shichifukujin pilgrimage''' located across '''seven temples in Ōta City, Tokyo'''. It was established in '''January 1981''' (Shōwa 56).<ref>Tsukada Yoshio, *Edo-Tokyo Pilgrimage Encyclopedia*, Shitamachi Times Publishing, 1989, pp. 171-172.</ref>

== List of Ikegami Shichifukujin ==
* '''{{ill|Ebisu (god)|ja|恵比寿神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu (god)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579198|1=Ebisu (mythology)}}''' - {{ill|Yōgen-ji (Ōta)|ja|養源寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-ji (Ōta)|1=養源寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666841}}
* '''{{ill|Jurōjin|en|Jurōjin|qid=Q389611|lt=Jurōjin|1=Jurōjin}}''' - {{ill|Shōei-in (Ōta)|ja|照栄院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōei-in (Ōta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579199|1=Shōei-in (Ōta)}} Myōkendō
* '''{{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}''' - {{ill|Gonjō-in (Ōta)|ja|厳定院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonjō-in (Ōta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579200|1=Gonjō-in (Ōta)}}
* '''{{ill|Fukurokuju|en|Fukurokuju|qid=Q780797|lt=Fukurokuju|1=Fukurokuju}}''' - {{ill|Honjō-in (Ōta)|ja|本成院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honjō-in (Ōta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579201|1=Honjō-in (Ōta)}}
* '''{{ill|Daikokuten|en|Daikokuten|qid=Q1759916|lt=Daikokuten|1=Daikokuten}}''' - {{ill|Honkō-ji (Ōta)|ja|本光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkō-ji (Ōta)|1=honkouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11519860}}
* '''{{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}}''' - {{ill|Ikegami Honmon-ji|en|Ikegami Honmon-ji|qid=Q1537312|lt=Ikegami Honmon-ji|1=Ikegami Honmon-ji}} Bimyō-an
* '''{{ill|Hotei (god)|ja|布袋尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotei (god)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 布袋|1=Budai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q599838}}''' - {{ill|Sōzen-ji (Ōta)|ja|曹禅寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōzen-ji (Ōta)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579202|1=Sōzen-ji (Ōta)}}

== Notes ==
=== About the Ikegami Shichifukujin Daikokuten
: Until December 2018, the '''Daikokuten''' of the '''Ikegami Shichifukujin''' was enshrined at '''{{ill|Batō Kannon-dō|ja|馬頭観音堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Batō Kannon-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579205|1=Batō Kannon-dō}}''', located near '''Ikegami Police Station'''. However, due to the aging of the hall, Daikokuten was temporarily moved while renovation was planned.
: From December 2018 to January 2020, '''Daikokuten was temporarily relocated''' to {{ill|Yōgen-ji (Ōta)|ja|養源寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōgen-ji (Ōta)|1=養源寺|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666841}}, where '''Ebisu''' is also enshrined.
: Although initially planned as a temporary move, it was later '''decided to permanently relocate Daikokuten to''' {{ill|Honkō-ji (Ōta)|ja|本光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkō-ji (Ōta)|1=honkouji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11519860}} in '''February 2020'''.
: As of '''October 2021''', the '''Ikegami Batō Kannon-dō''' remains inaccessible.


== See Also ==
* {{ill|Seven Lucky Gods|en|Seven Lucky Gods|qid=Q846858|lt=Shichifukujin|1=Seven Lucky Gods}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* Group Manpo (ed.), *Relaxed & Leisurely Edo-Tokyo Shichifukujin Pilgrimage*, Nihon Shuppansha, 2004, pp. 56-59.
* Shichifukujin Pilgrimage Association, *Shutoken Shichifukujin Pilgrimage Beneficial Course Guide*, Meits Publishing, 2011, pp. 54-57.
* Tsukada Yoshio, *Edo-Tokyo Pilgrimage Encyclopedia*, Shitamachi Times Publishing, 1989, pp. 171-172.

== Additional Literature ==
* {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}}|volume=Vol. 43, Ebara District No. 5|chapter=Tokumochi Village * '''Bimyō-an / Bishamonten-dō'''|id={{NDLJP|763981/47}}|publisher=Ministry of Home Affairs Geographic Bureau|date=1884-6}}
